resources.string: Show exact cents, percentages and measurements everywhere. Off keeps the app in plain language.
How much time you get to read the prompt and find the note. Scoring is equally fair at every pace, and your bests carry over when you move up.
Up to %1$d s to start each note.
How forgiving the scoring is when a note isn\'t perfectly in tune.
How many notes in one round. Scores only compare within the same length.
Whatever your teacher uses — notes are written this way everywhere.
Sometimes show La♯ as Si♭ — the same note has two names, and this way you learn both. Off keeps everything written as sharps.
The A your orchestra tunes to. Leave at 440 unless your teacher says otherwise.
In the Chords game, a note can often be played several ways — open or fingered in your positions. This is how the game chooses (open strings are never scored).
Chime when you land a note, buzz when you miss — so you can keep your eyes on the fingerboard.
Your phone\'s media volume is muted — game sounds stay silent no matter what this slider says.
Warns when everything you play starts leaning sharp or flat, so you can reset your ear instead of practicing wrong notes.

Room check measures your room\'s background noise (quick — run it when you practice somewhere new). Full setup teaches the app how your bass sounds on this phone (run it once, or with a new phone or a new bass).
Full setup (new phone or new bass)
Noise gate: sound below this level is ignored as room noise (gate at level %1$d / 100 — Room check sets it automatically).
Right note, wrong octave still counts
Low notes can fool the microphone by a whole octave. Keep this on so that never costs you points — the app scores how in tune you were instead.
Export your whole practice history to a file you can keep, then restore it on a new phone. Nothing is stored in the cloud — this file is your only copy.
Backup created — save it somewhere safe.
Merge keeps what\'s on this phone and adds any rounds from the backup that aren\'t already here. Replace wipes this phone\'s history first.
This permanently deletes all history, personal bests and achievements on this phone, then loads the backup. This can\'t be undone.

Records your rounds — the sound and what the app heard — so that when something seems wrong (a note not picked up, a weird score), you can email the report to the developer and the app gets fixed. Leave off for normal play.
